title: Ethiopian Tikil Gomen (Spiced Cabbage and Potato)

ingredients.name: cabbage (firm, white or green), cut into 4 cm chunks

subs.name: Pointed cabbage (savoy) or even pak choi; cauliflower florets give a denser result

ingredients.name: potato, peeled and cut into 2 cm cubes

subs.name: Sweet potato (shakarkand) for a slightly sweeter, more nutritious stew
Green beans or peas added in the last 5 minutes for colour and crunch
Ghee for a richer flavour (no longer vegan); adds a nuttiness that is excellent with the turmeric

steps.text: Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pot over medium flame. Add the sliced onion and cook for 5 minutes until translucent. Add garlic and ginger; stir for 1 minute until the raw smell disappears.
Add turmeric, cumin, coriander powder and black pepper. Stir for 30 seconds until the spices coat the onion and the colour turns a vivid golden-yellow.
Add the diced potato and carrot. Stir well to coat in the spiced oil. Pour in 100 ml water, add salt, cover and cook on medium-low for 8 minutes until the potato begins to soften.

Add the cabbage chunks and stir gently to mix. The cabbage will release its own water. Cover and cook for 10 minutes on low heat, stirring once halfway, until the cabbage is tender and the potato is completely soft and coated in the yellow-spiced sauce.

Uncover and stir. If any liquid remains, increase heat briefly to evaporate it — the finished tikil gomen should be lightly sauced, not soupy. Taste for salt and serve hot over rice or alongside injera.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: cabbage → Pointed cabbage (savoy) or even pak choi; cauliflower florets give a denser result; potato → Sweet potato (shakarkand) for a slightly sweeter, more nutritious stew; carrot → Green beans or peas added in the last 5 minutes for colour and crunch; oil → Ghee for a richer flavour (no longer vegan); adds a nuttiness that is excellent with the turmeric
indian_kitchen_adaptation: This dish is remarkably similar to gobhi aloo (Indian cabbage and potato stir-fry) with turmeric and cumin — any Indian cook will feel at home. The only difference is the absence of mustard seed tempering and curry leaves. Cook in a kadai with a lid. All ingredients are standard Indian pantry items and the dish can be on the table in 40 minutes.
